STATE MANAGEMENT
With the state module you will learn about:
Ôāß The essentials of State Management ‚Äď Mental Focus, Physiology and Emotions
Ôāß The Ideal States
Ôāß The four empowering states
Ôāß The disempowering states
Ôāß The 5 modalities of state ‚Äď Vision, Auditory, Kinesthetic, Gustatory, and Olfactory
Ôāß Setting Anchors to trigger a specific state
Introduction
Introduction to State Management
Introduction
The ability to manage your emotional state of mind drives everything you do. There are certain
states you want to be able to trigger in yourself before you begin any sale and there are also certain
states you want to be able to avoid, like the feeling of massive uncertainty, when you‚Äôre about to
close a sale. Once you master your own states, you will be able to use this same concept to
influence other people.
This module is about managing your physiological and emotional state, no matter what is going

-- 1 of 9 --

on around you at the time, so then you can access all the resources (e.g. skills and training) you
have to be successful in that sale.
Your state dictates how you operate in the moment. There are a number of ‚Äėpositive‚Äô states that
lead to wealth and success, and then there are ‚Äėnegative‚Äô states that are disempowering and will
undermine your success as a salesperson. These will be explored in more depth later in this module.
Positive States are:
Ôā∑ Certainty
Ôā∑ Clarity
Ôā∑ Confidence
Ôā∑ Courage
Negative States are:
Ôā∑ Uncertainty
Ôā∑ Fear
Ôā∑ Greed
Ôā∑ Overwhelm
Essentials of State Management
3 Elements

-- 2 of 9 --

There are 3 elements to state management:
Ôā∑ Mental Focus
Ôā∑ Physiology
Ôā∑ Emotions
You change or create a state by changing these three elements ‚ÄĒ what you choose to focus on, the
movement of your body, and the emotion you create as a result of that.
If you choose to focus on negative aspects of your life, and match your physiology (e.g. head
down), you are going to end up feeling disempowered and uncertain about yourself, and will
ultimately end up attracting more negativity into your life.
If you choose to focus on positive aspects of your life, and match your physiology (e.g. smiling),
you will feel more empowered and certain about yourself, and ultimately attract positive things
into your life.

-- 3 of 9 --

The Ideal States: The Four C‚Äôs
4 C's
There are Four Ideal States: The Four C‚Äôs:
There are certain states that empower you and certain emotional states that disempower you.
Empowering states can help you to achieve wealth and success, whereas negative states can lead
to failure and poverty.
There are four empowering states that are called the 4 C‚Äôs:
Ôā∑ Certainty
Ôā∑ Clarity
Ôā∑ Confidence
Ôā∑ Courage
Four Ideal States: The Four C‚Äôs:
When you have total clarity about what you‚Äôre doing, you‚Äôre confident you can succeed, you have
the courage to put yourself on the line, and you‚Äôre in a state of total certainty that you can make it
happen. In this state you become a force of nature and you can influence just about anyone you
get in front of.
When you are in any one, or ideally more than one of these empowered states your best physical
and mental resources are available to you. You‚Äôre able to maximize your potential in the moment.
Conversely, in a disempowered state, these resources tend to disappear.

-- 4 of 9 --

Four Disempowering States:
Just as there are four empowering states, there are also four disempowering states.
Ôā∑ Doubt
Ôā∑ Fear
Ôā∑ Uncertainty
Ôā∑ Overwhelm
The key to being effective in sales, and in any area of your life, is first to be aware of the effect
your state has on your behaviour and what states you tend to live in. In addition you need to be
able to trigger the states you want in yourself and your prospect to maximize your results. When
you feel certainty in your product you transfer this certainty to your prospects. This is called
emotional transference.
Managing Your State
The 5 Modalities Of State
5 Modalities of State
The key to managing your state is to identify the modalities that influence you, so you can use
them to create strong, positive states. The way we process the world around us, how we make
sense of the world, is through our five senses. And, these five senses are called your modalities.

-- 5 of 9 --

THE 5 MODALITIES OF STATE
The KEY to managing your state is to understand and identify the modalities that influence you
and others.

-- 6 of 9 --

Setting Anchors
NLP and Anchoring
Neurolinguistic Programming
NLP refers to the association between the neurological processes in the brain (‚Äėneuro‚Äô), how we
use language (‚Äėlinguistic‚Äô), and our patterns of behaviour that have been learned through
experience (‚Äėprogramming‚Äô).
Setting Anchors
The easiest way to train your body to go into the empowering states you want to be in can best be
accomplished through the use of anchors. Anchors are learned associations between a specific state
and a very specific trigger.
You can set anchors for yourself even if you‚Äôre feeling uncertain and disempowered. In the key
moments when you need to change your state‚ÄĒlike in a sales situation‚ÄĒyou can trigger a state of
certainty. The more you fire off your anchor, the easier it gets to fire it off again and again.
It‚Äôs important to remember, you can‚Äôt set an anchor if you‚Äôre in a state that‚Äôs less than ten out of
ten. You have to get to the highest level of the state and in the exact moment when you‚Äôre in a
‚Äúpeak state‚ÄĚ, that is when you set the anchor.
Line up what you focus on with how you move your body to generate an emotion that happens
automatically. This is called creating a high-level ‚ÄėPeak‚Äô or performance state. This can be any
state: certainty, clarity, confidence, or courage.
More specifically, there are certain movements we might find ourselves doing automatically when
in certain states. For example, when we feel confident we stand tall and might make certain hand
movements (e.g. make a fist). We can use these movements or gestures as our ‚Äėanchor‚Äô.

-- 7 of 9 --

When you combine this anchoring technique with the outer world skills of the Straight Line ‚Äď
you‚Äôre unstoppable. Having both creates a powerful combination in terms of the results you can
produce.
Anchoring Exercise
Anchoring Exercise
This exercise may seem strange at first and will take you out of your comfort zone. You may want
to do this in a private and familiar environment. Also, you will need to do this exercise with your
eyes closed.
Focus again on the experience you described previously when you felt absolute certainty about
something. You may have had absolute clarity about a solution to a problem or answer to a
question. Recall more details about this experience in terms of what you see, hear, smell, feel, and
taste. Say ‚Äėyes‚Äô repeatedly as you recall this experience, and as you feel more certain say ‚Äėyes‚Äô
louder and do your associated anchoring move. Continue until you feel 100% certainty.

-- 8 of 9 --

State Management Close
Close
The key to success with the state management process, is to keep practicing it every day until it
becomes habitual.
The anchoring exercise may feel unnatural to begin with, but once you start to see the positive
impact it has on your prospects, you will be prepared to look a little weird in the short term, as it
leads to your success and wealth.
Practice, practice and more practice is really the key message here.

-- 9 of 9 --
